# services.frigate

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.frigate`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.frigate.checkConfig` | `boolean` | Whether to check the configuration at build time. |
| `services.frigate.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Frigate NVR. |
| `services.frigate.hostname` | `string` | Hostname of the nginx vhost to configure. Only nginx is supported by upstream for direct reverse proxying. |
| `services.frigate.package` | `package` | The frigate package to use. |
| `services.frigate.preCheckConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | This script gets run before the config is checked. It can be used to, e.g., set environment variables needed or transform the config (available as `$out`) to make it checkable in the sandbox. |
| `services.frigate.settings` | `open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value)` | Frigate configuration as a nix attribute set. See the project documentation for how to configure frigate. - [Creating a config file](https://docs.frigate.video/guides/getting_started) - [Configuration reference](https://docs.frigate.video/configuration/index) |
| `services.frigate.settings.cameras` | `attribute set of (open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value))` | Attribute set of cameras configurations. <https://docs.frigate.video/configuration/cameras> |
| `services.frigate.settings.cameras.<name>.ffmpeg.inputs` | `list of (open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value))` | List of inputs for this camera. |
| `services.frigate.settings.cameras.<name>.ffmpeg.inputs.*.path` | `string` | Stream URL |
| `services.frigate.settings.cameras.<name>.ffmpeg.inputs.*.roles` | `list of (one of "audio", "detect", "record")` | List of roles for this stream |
| `services.frigate.settings.database.path` | `absolute path` | Path to the SQLite database used |
| `services.frigate.settings.ffmpeg.path` | `string or package convertible to it` | Package providing the ffmpeg and ffprobe executables below the bin/ directory. |
| `services.frigate.settings.mqtt.enabled` | `boolean` | Whether to enable MQTT support. |
| `services.frigate.settings.mqtt.host` | `null or string` | MQTT server hostname |
| `services.frigate.vaapiDriver` | `null or one of "i965", "iHD", "nouveau", "vdpau", "nvidia", "radeonsi"` | Force usage of a particular VA-API driver for video acceleration. Use together with `settings.ffmpeg.hwaccel_args`. Setting this *is not required* for VA-API to work, but it can help steer VA-API towards the correct card if you have multiple. :::{.note} For VA-API to work you must enable {option}`hardware.graphics.enable` (sufficient for AMDGPU) and pass for example `pkgs.intel-media-driver` (required for Intel 5th Gen. and newer) into {option}`hardware.graphics.extraPackages`. ::: See also: - <https://docs.frigate.video/configuration/hardware_acceleration> - <https://docs.frigate.video/configuration/ffmpeg_presets#hwaccel-presets> |
